admission_test_type: Master-level evaluation of curricular requirements, academic record, portfolio, and language requirements; no dedicated written entrance test is published for this programme.
applicant_route: EU qualification with a non-Italian Bachelor's degree
Non-EU residing abroad and requiring a visa
admission_call_round: Italian qualification / Architecture Area: first-semester admission only; Apply@polito applications open 4 June 2026 and close 18 August 2026; portfolio-evaluation results are published on 3 September 2026.
Non-Italian qualification: 1st call opens 19 December 2025 and closes 13 February 2026 at 2:00 p.m. Italian time; results on 15 April 2026.
Non-Italian qualification: 2nd call opens 18 March 2026 and closes 20 April 2026 at 2:00 p.m. Italian time; results on 22 June 2026.
This programme is admitted only in the first semester.
application_platform: Apply@polito. Non-EU applicants residing abroad and requiring a visa must also complete study-visa pre-enrolment on UNIVERSITALY.

academic_requirements.general_eligibility: Applicants must hold a Bachelor's degree (EQF level 6) or another equivalent qualification that gives access to second-cycle studies.
academic_requirements.programme_background: The A.Y. 2026/2027 course-specific requirements PDF lists Architecture as the recommended background for Architecture for Sustainability.
academic_requirements.curricular_requirements: For the Architecture Area route, a curricular requirement is the possession of a Bachelor's degree in class L-17 (or class 4 under D.M. 509/99), or another suitable foreign qualification recognized under art. 6 c. 2 of D.M. 270/04. Applicants must also have completed the compulsory courses and activities listed in the table of class L-17 of the Bachelor's degree programmes in Architecture.
academic_requirements.other_universities: If the Bachelor's degree was awarded by a university other than Politecnico di Torino, applicants must have a weighted average grade equal to or greater than 24/30 and must submit a portfolio through Apply@polito.
academic_requirements.foreign_qualification_route: Applicants with a non-Italian qualification are assessed by an Academic Committee for consistency between their previous university studies and the programme's curricular requirements. For this programme, both syllabus/course descriptions and portfolio are required.

country_specific_eligibility: No Turkey-specific exception was published in the checked official sources. Turkish applicants follow the general non-Italian qualification route and, if residing abroad, must also complete UNIVERSITALY pre-enrolment for a study visa.
minimum_background_or_credits: Recommended background: Architecture. For the Architecture Area route, the mandatory curricular background is an L-17/class 4 Architecture-type degree or another suitable equivalent qualification, plus the compulsory class L-17 activities. Applicants with a non-Italian qualification who have not yet graduated from a long-cycle degree may apply if they have at least 15 years of schooling, at least 3 university years, and at least 180 ECTS credits or equivalent.

language_requirements.english_pathway: For the English-language pathway, English level B2 or higher is required. The 2026/2027 Course-Specific Requirements table gives IELTS 5.5 as the minimum reference score for Architecture for Sustainability.
language_requirements.italian_pathway: For the Italian/partially Italian route, the course-specific table lists Italian B2 plus IELTS 5.5.
language_requirements.accepted_certificates: Accepted English and Italian certificates are listed in PoliTO's official certificate charts on the admissions pages.
language_requirements.exemptions: Official English-language exemption cases are listed on the non-Italian-qualification page, including specified English-medium qualifications and certain country/qualification combinations.

english_certificate_codes: For TOEFL/TOEIC certificates, applicants must indicate PoliTO code C285. For Cambridge certificates, applicants must share the results with Politecnico di Torino and provide the Candidate ID number.
language_submission_timing: Applicants with a non-Italian qualification must upload the language certificate within the Apply@polito application. Applicants with an Italian qualification may submit the application before obtaining the certificate, but they must satisfy the language requirement by the enrolment stage or follow the single-courses route for unfulfilled language requirements.

application_deadline_eu.programme_note: Architecture for Sustainability is open only in the first semester.
application_deadline_eu.italian_qualification_route: Apply@polito applications open 4 June 2026 and close 18 August 2026; portfolio-evaluation results are published on 3 September 2026.
application_deadline_eu.non_italian_qualification_no_visa_route_: 19 December 2025 to 13 February 2026 at 2:00 p.m. Italian time; results on 15 April 2026.
18 March 2026 to 20 April 2026 at 2:00 p.m. Italian time; results on 22 June 2026.

application_deadline_non_eu.programme_note: Architecture for Sustainability is open only in the first semester.
application_deadline_non_eu.first_call: 19 December 2025 to 13 February 2026 at 2:00 p.m. Italian time; results on 15 April 2026.
application_deadline_non_eu.second_call: 18 March 2026 to 20 April 2026 at 2:00 p.m. Italian time; results on 22 June 2026.
application_deadline_non_eu.enrolment_deadline: Online enrolment for the first semester closes on 5 October 2026.
application_deadline_non_eu.visa_document_deadline: The deadline to upload the study visa (type D) is 30 November 2026.

universitaly_preenrolment_deadline: For non-EU applicants residing abroad and requiring a visa: 30 June 2026 for 1st-call admissions and 15 July 2026 for 2nd-call admissions.
application_fee: EUR 50 non-refundable application fee for applicants with a non-Italian qualification. The 2026/2027 Architecture Area page also states that a fee is required for applicants from other Italian universities, but it does not specify the amount. [uncertain]
required_documents: Passport, or ID card for EU nationals
Residence permit for non-EU citizens living in Italy
Bachelor's degree certificate, or an official university document stating the provisional graduation date if not yet graduated
English and/or Italian language certificate, depending on the chosen route
Declaration of Value of the Bachelor's degree or CIMEA Statement of Comparability (compulsory for enrolment if applying with a foreign qualification)
Study visa type D for non-EU applicants residing abroad at enrolment stage
document_translation_authentication: Documents must be issued on official letterheaded paper, stamped and signed by the issuing institution. Documents in Italian, English, French, or Spanish are accepted. If documents are in another language, the original document and an official English or Italian translation must be merged into a single file. For enrolment, PoliTO requires official translation and foreign-qualification recognition documentation such as a Declaration of Value or a CIMEA/ENIC-NARIC comparability document where applicable.

entry_exam_or_test.written_test: No dedicated written entrance exam is published for Architecture for Sustainability.
entry_exam_or_test.selection_method: Admission is based on evaluation of curricular requirements, academic record, portfolio, and language requirements.
entry_exam_or_test.portfolio_rule: Applicants from universities other than Politecnico di Torino must upload a portfolio; for applicants with a non-Italian qualification, both syllabus/course descriptions and portfolio are required for this programme.
entry_exam_or_test.result_publication: For Italian-qualification applicants in the Architecture Area, portfolio-evaluation results are published in Apply@polito on 3 September 2026.

test_score_or_threshold: For applicants from universities other than Politecnico di Torino, the published academic threshold is a weighted average grade equal to or greater than 24/30, together with portfolio evaluation. For the English-language pathway, the minimum language reference is IELTS 5.5 / CEFR B2 or equivalent.

source_quotes.quote: Uncapped number of students, evaluation of the admission requirements
Degree code: LM-4: ARCHITECTURE AND ARCHITECTURAL ENGINEERING
taught both in Italian (with the possibility to choose some English-taught courses) and in English.
ARCHITECTURE FOR SUSTAINABILITY English IELTS 5.5 Architecture Both required
ARCHITETTURA PER LA SOSTENIBILITÀ partially in Italian/ partially in English Italian B2 and IELTS 5.5 Architecture Both required
weighted average grade of all the exams equal to or greater than 24/30
Pre-enrolment deadline for Study Visa 30 June 2026

uncertainty_notes: The programme is not fully English because the official catalogue page lists the overall teaching language as 'Italian, English' and the programme-details page says it is taught both in Italian and in English.

has_complete_english_track is set to true because the official curriculum exposes a dedicated 'Percorso in lingua inglese' with Year 1 and Year 2 study plans in English, including Year-2 courses such as 'Adaptive public spaces'.
english_track_name is translated into English from the official Italian label 'Percorso in lingua inglese', so the field is marked [uncertain].

The EUR 50 application fee is explicit for applicants with a non-Italian qualification. The 2026/2027 Architecture Area page also says a fee is required for applicants from other Italian universities, but it does not state the amount, so application_fee is marked [uncertain].
